clusters.title: Trump and Iran trade new threats after strikes exchanged
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump and Iran trade new threats after strikes exchanged
bullet_summary: The US launched strikes against Iran in the early hours of Wednesday, in retaliation for what it said was Iran’s downing of a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz
Reported by BBC World, France24, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Trump and Iran trade new threats after strikes exchanged
items.standfirst: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.
items.content: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

items.title: US strikes Iran after blaming Tehran for helicopter crash
items.standfirst: The United States launched airstrikes early Wednesday against Iran after blaming Tehran for the crash of an American attack helicopter, and Iran fired back at countries in the region — another escalation that threatened to derail efforts to end the war. Hours after Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan came under Iranian fire, U.S. President Donald Trump posted on his his Truth Social platform that Iran was taking “too long to negotiate a deal” and that “now they will have to pay the price.”
items.content: The United States launched airstrikes early Wednesday against Iran after blaming Tehran for the crash of an American attack helicopter, and Iran fired back at countries in the region — another escalation that threatened to derail efforts to end the war. Hours after Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan came under Iranian fire, U.S. President Donald Trump posted on his his Truth Social platform that Iran was taking “too long to negotiate a deal” and that “now they will have to pay the price.”

items.title: US bombs Iran’s water facilities: Why that’s so significant
items.standfirst: The US struck several sites in Iran, saying it retaliated against the downing of a US helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz
items.content: The US struck several sites in Iran, saying it retaliated against the downing of a US helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: U.S. launches strikes on Iran after downing of Apache helicopter
items.standfirst: The U.S. retaliated against Iran, launching widespread strikes after Iran shot down an Apache helicopter. Iran has since launched its own retaliatory strikes. Charlie D'Agata reports.
items.content: The U.S. retaliated against Iran, launching widespread strikes after Iran shot down an Apache helicopter. Iran has since launched its own retaliatory strikes. Charlie D'Agata reports.

items.title: Trump warns Iran will ‘pay the price’ for delay in striking peace deal
items.standfirst: US president’s remarks come after Washington and Tehran exchange fire following downing of American helicopter
items.content: US president’s remarks come after Washington and Tehran exchange fire following downing of American helicopter

items.title: Middle East peace talks in doubt as Iran says it needs to ‘reassess’ after overnight strikes
items.standfirst: US launches strikes in retaliation for downing of US army helicopter, while White House source says deal could still be closeThe future of peace talks in the Middle East have been thrown into question after Iran’s foreign ministry said it needed to “reassess” its participation, while Donald Trump said Iran would have to “pay the price” after the two countries traded fire overnight, drawing neighbouring states back into an on-and-off war that has consumed the region since late February.The US launched strikes against Iran in the early hours of Wednesday, in retaliation for what it said was Iran’s downing of a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz. Iran then launched a wave of retaliatory airstrikes claiming hits on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. Continue reading...
items.content: US launches strikes in retaliation for downing of US army helicopter, while White House source says deal could still be closeThe future of peace talks in the Middle East have been thrown into question after Iran’s foreign ministry said it needed to “reassess” its participation, while Donald Trump said Iran would have to “pay the price” after the two countries traded fire overnight, drawing neighbouring states back into an on-and-off war that has consumed the region since late February.The US launched strikes against Iran in the early hours of Wednesday, in retaliation for what it said was Iran’s downing of a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz. Iran then launched a wave of retaliatory airstrikes claiming hits on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump launches strikes against Iran after downing of US army helicopter
items.standfirst: US president blames Tehran for loss of Apache gunship, whose crew were rescued by a drone near strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US has launched strikes against Iran after Donald Trump blamed Tehran for downing a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz, imperilling a shaky ceasefire that was announced by the two countries in April.The attacks triggered a wave of retaliatory strikes from Iran on Wednesday morning, with Tehran saying it had targeted Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. Continue reading...
items.content: US president blames Tehran for loss of Apache gunship, whose crew were rescued by a drone near strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe US has launched strikes against Iran after Donald Trump blamed Tehran for downing a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz, imperilling a shaky ceasefire that was announced by the two countries in April.The attacks triggered a wave of retaliatory strikes from Iran on Wednesday morning, with Tehran saying it had targeted Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Anti-immigration protesters torch buildings and vehicles, block roads over Belfast stabbing
clusters.neutral_headline: Why has Belfast erupted in anti-immigrant violence after a knife attack
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC Top Stories, NBC World and others

items.title: Anti-immigration protesters torch buildings and vehicles, block roads over Belfast stabbing
items.standfirst: Anti-immigration protesters torched buildings and vehicles in Belfast on Tuesday evening and blocked roads, a day after a stabbing allegedly by a Sudanese refugee, captured in a graphic video that shocked the country. Hundreds of protesters, many masked, gathered at several locations across Belfast, AFP journalists saw. A bus and several cars were set alight, while a building fringing the city centre caught fire and its residents had to be evacuated. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.
items.content: Anti-immigration protesters torched buildings and vehicles in Belfast on Tuesday evening and blocked roads, a day after a stabbing allegedly by a Sudanese refugee, captured in a graphic video that shocked the country. Hundreds of protesters, many masked, gathered at several locations across Belfast, AFP journalists saw. A bus and several cars were set alight, while a building fringing the city centre caught fire and its residents had to be evacuated. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.

items.title: Belfast stabbing suspect due in court after night of anti-immigration violence
items.standfirst: A Sudanese man was due to appear in court in Northern Ireland Wednesday over a brutal knife attack captured on video that triggered a night of violence by anti-immigration protesters. Demonstrators torched buildings and vehicles, and blocked roads in Belfast on Tuesday evening, a day after the stabbing, allegedly by a refugee originally from Sudan, shocked the United Kingdom.
items.content: A Sudanese man was due to appear in court in Northern Ireland Wednesday over a brutal knife attack captured on video that triggered a night of violence by anti-immigration protesters. Demonstrators torched buildings and vehicles, and blocked roads in Belfast on Tuesday evening, a day after the stabbing, allegedly by a refugee originally from Sudan, shocked the United Kingdom.

items.title: What we know about Belfast attack and disorder
items.standfirst: Houses across the city have been set on fire following a knife attack earlier this week.
items.content: Houses across the city have been set on fire following a knife attack earlier this week.

items.title: Belfast: Anti-immigrant violence erupts after knife attack
items.standfirst: Masked men set fire to homes and torched cars and buses in a wave of anti-immigrant violence in Belfast on Tuesday night, following a knife attack allegedly by a Sudanese refugee in the city.
items.content: Masked men set fire to homes and torched cars and buses in a wave of anti-immigrant violence in Belfast on Tuesday night, following a knife attack allegedly by a Sudanese refugee in the city.

items.title: Homes engulfed in flames as Belfast unrest continues
items.standfirst: Homes in Belfast were engulfed in flames as unrest continued after police charged a Sudanese man in a knife attack that seriously injured one person.
items.content: Homes in Belfast were engulfed in flames as unrest continued after police charged a Sudanese man in a knife attack that seriously injured one person.

items.title: Why has Belfast erupted in anti-immigrant violence after a knife attack?
items.standfirst: Ministers have blamed far-right online agitators for stoking racial tension following a stabbing.
items.content: Ministers have blamed far-right online agitators for stoking racial tension following a stabbing.

items.title: Anti-immigrant violence erupts across Belfast after knife attack
items.standfirst: Masked men burned families out of their homes in Belfast and torched ​a number of vehicles in a wave of anti-immigrant violence on Tuesday night that followed a knife attack by a Sudanese man. Hundreds of protesters, many with their faces covered, attacked police and burned vehicles in a number of locations across Northern Ireland after a video of the knife attack, which left one person with serious neck and head wounds, went viral. FRANCE 24's Hervé Amoric reports from Belfast.
items.content: Masked men burned families out of their homes in Belfast and torched ​a number of vehicles in a wave of anti-immigrant violence on Tuesday night that followed a knife attack by a Sudanese man. Hundreds of protesters, many with their faces covered, attacked police and burned vehicles in a number of locations across Northern Ireland after a video of the knife attack, which left one person with serious neck and head wounds, went viral. FRANCE 24's Hervé Amoric reports from Belfast.

items.title: Protesters set fire to bus as unrest continues after knife attack
items.standfirst: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Belfast, some setting vehicles on fire, after police charged a Sudanese man in a knife attack that seriously injured one person.
items.content: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Belfast, some setting vehicles on fire, after police charged a Sudanese man in a knife attack that seriously injured one person.

items.title: Violent anti-immigrant protests flare up across Belfast after knife attack
items.standfirst: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.
items.content: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.

clusters.title: Pilot accused of flying 900 flights without valid license
clusters.neutral_headline: Pilot accused of flying 900 flights without valid license
bullet_summary: The man used false documents after being promoted to captain in 2009 until his retirement last year, authorities said
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Pilot accused of flying 900 flights without valid license
items.standfirst: An Air Canada pilot is accused of flying 900 flights over 17 years without a proper license. The man used false documents after being promoted to captain in 2009 until his retirement last year, authorities said. He did have a valid commercial pilot license, but never got the license required to act as a captain.
items.content: An Air Canada pilot is accused of flying 900 flights over 17 years without a proper license. The man used false documents after being promoted to captain in 2009 until his retirement last year, authorities said. He did have a valid commercial pilot license, but never got the license required to act as a captain.

items.title: Former Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license for 16 years
items.standfirst: Geoffrey Wall is alleged to have flown over 900 flights domestically and internationally between 2009 and 2025A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police have said.Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel regional police. Continue reading...
items.content: Geoffrey Wall is alleged to have flown over 900 flights domestically and internationally between 2009 and 2025A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police have said.Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel regional police. clusters.title: Somali referee denied US visa gets hero’s welcome back home, vows to attend next World Cup
clusters.neutral_headline: Hero’s welcome for Somali referee denied entry to US World Cup tournament
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Somali referee denied US visa gets hero’s welcome back home, vows to attend next World Cup
items.standfirst: Hundreds of fans, government and football officials gathered at Mogadishu's airport Wednesday to welcome Omar Artan after the Somali refree was denied entry to the US for the World Cup. Somalia's dream of having its first national officiate a World Cup may have been dashed, but Artan told his supporters not to give up hope and vowed to participate in the next FIFA tournament in 2030.
items.content: Hundreds of fans, government and football officials gathered at Mogadishu's airport Wednesday to welcome Omar Artan after the Somali refree was denied entry to the US for the World Cup. Somalia's dream of having its first national officiate a World Cup may have been dashed, but Artan told his supporters not to give up hope and vowed to participate in the next FIFA tournament in 2030.

items.title: Hero’s welcome for Somali referee denied entry to US World Cup tournament
items.standfirst: The first Somali picked to referee a World Cup rose from Mogadishu's pitches, only to be barred at the US border.
items.content: The first Somali picked to referee a World Cup rose from Mogadishu's pitches, only to be barred at the US border.

items.title: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US arrives home to hero's welcome
items.standfirst: After being denied entry to the US, Somali referee Omar Artan was given a hero’s welcome upon his return to Somalia. Large numbers of supporters and Somali government officials received him enthusiastically at the airport in the capital, Mogadishu.
items.content: After being denied entry to the US, Somali referee Omar Artan was given a hero’s welcome upon his return to Somalia. Large numbers of supporters and Somali government officials received him enthusiastically at the airport in the capital, Mogadishu.

items.title: Latest news on Graham Platner and Nancy Mace's political bids
items.standfirst: Primary races in four states occurred on Tuesday. Republican Rep. Nancy Mace conceded her bid for South Carolina governor, and Graham Platner won the Maine Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.
items.content: Primary races in four states occurred on Tuesday. Republican Rep. Nancy Mace conceded her bid for South Carolina governor, and Graham Platner won the Maine Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

items.title: Graham Platner wins Maine primary election: Results and key takeaways
items.standfirst: Platner wins in Maine, Republican Nancy Mace loses in S Carolina. Results for Nevada and North Carolina also announced.
items.content: Platner wins in Maine, Republican Nancy Mace loses in S Carolina. Results for Nevada and North Carolina also announced.

items.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes. And, House passes ICE and Border Patrol funding
items.standfirst: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.
items.content: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.(Image credit: Ryan Murphy)

items.standfirst: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.
items.content: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

items.title: Pakistan renews air strikes on Afghanistan, killing dozens
items.standfirst: Pakistan on Wednesday launched deadly air strikes on Afghanistan after a period of relative calm following months of fighting at the beginning of the year. Pakistan's government said 26 "militants" were killed while Afghan authorities said at least 12 people, including children, died in provinces near the border.
items.content: Pakistan on Wednesday launched deadly air strikes on Afghanistan after a period of relative calm following months of fighting at the beginning of the year. Pakistan's government said 26 "militants" were killed while Afghan authorities said at least 12 people, including children, died in provinces near the border.

items.content: Claude Fable 5 is a version of Anthropic's Claude Mythos, an AI program which caused a stir among technology, finance, and government leaders.

items.content: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.
